citation_short: Seoul Metropolitan Government, History of Seoul
title: History, historical place of Seoul
pages_or_sections: Contemporary Seoul section, lines 380-385 in fetched page
strengths: Official city source with a clear wartime population and outflow summary.
limitations: Not an audit-grade casualty dataset and does not separate direct from indirect mortality.
notes: Useful for proposed episodeization and denominator caution, not for a final direct-death numerator.

citation_short: Jeong 2008 on South Korean civilian casualty statistics
title: 한국전쟁기 남한 민간인 인명피해 조사의 유형과 특징－한국정부의 통계 · 명부를 중심으로－
pages_or_sections: Abstract lines 43-46 and 109-110 in fetched page
strengths: Explains what South Korean government casualty registers counted and highlights cause-of-death categories.
limitations: National overview; does not itself provide a clean Seoul episode numerator with person-matched denominator.
notes: Important for understanding why Seoul sources mix death, missing, and abduction registers and why direct deaths need careful parsing.

citation_short: TRCK decision summary on Wolmido bombing
title: 월미도 미군폭격 사건
author_or_org: Truth and Reconciliation Commission, Republic of Korea
pages_or_sections: Decision text items 1-5, especially lines 154-158 in fetched page
strengths: Official case determination with explicit attacker identification and an estimate of actual civilian victims beyond the small set of named victims.
limitations: Identified victims are incomplete; estimated actual toll is rounded and partly inferential.
notes: Core civilian numerator source for the Wolmido episode.

title: 과거사 - 월미도 미군폭격 사건
author_or_org: Truth and Reconciliation Commission, Republic of Korea
strengths: Provides unusually useful micro-geographic and exposure details: households, residents, garrison size, timing of civilian departure, and attack pattern.
limitations: Narrative synthesis rather than a full technical appendix; laborer deaths remain unquantified.
notes: Main denominator source for Wolmido civilian person-months and garrison floor.

citation_short: USMC aviation history, Corsairs to Panthers
title: Corsairs to Panthers: U.S. Marine Aviation in Korea
author_or_org: U.S. Marine Corps University / USMC history program
pages_or_sections: PDF page 14-15, especially lines 1174-1188 in fetched text
strengths: Official military narrative with explicit counted enemy dead, prisoners, and the 'at least 150 more entombed' lower-bound statement.
limitations: Operational history focused on Marine performance; enemy dead may be conservative in some dimensions and optimistic in others, and the 'entombed' figure is indirect.
notes: Core military numerator source for the Wolmido episode.

citation_short: The Sea Services in the Korean War
title: The Sea Services in the Korean War 1950-1953
pages_or_sections: PDF page 143-144, especially lines 5554-5558 in fetched text
strengths: Confirms the 400-man garrison framing and the Marine casualty figure from another official military-history stream.
limitations: Again, not designed for civilian-harm accounting or denominator reconstruction.
notes: Useful cross-check on the Wolmido military side.

citation_short: U.S. Army No Gun Ri Review Report
title: U.S. Department of the Army No Gun Ri Review Report
strengths: Strongest single source for the micro-geography, refugee movement, and the presence/timing of 7th Cavalry battalions near the double overpass.
limitations: Conservative and contested on culpability and civilian casualty totals; no clean combatant-death count for the strict micro-episode.
notes: Core denominator and contextual source for No Gun Ri; not sufficient by itself for a final civilian death estimate.

citation_short: Hanley 2015 on No Gun Ri Peace Park account
title: In the Face of American Amnesia, The Grim Truths of No Gun Ri Find a Home
author_or_org: Charles J. Hanley / The Asia-Pacific Journal
strengths: Summarizes the No Gun Ri Peace Park's public account and gives a clear 250-300 deaths range with explicit U.S. air-and-ground attribution.
limitations: Secondary synthesis; not itself the original victim registry or government inquest.
notes: Used as the higher civilian death source for No Gun Ri.

citation_short: National Archives of Korea topic page on No Gun Ri
title: 노근리사건 대책
strengths: Official South Korean archival summary that preserves the joint-investigation framing and the registered casualties figure of 248.
limitations: The 248 is a reported casualty total, not a clean direct-death count; it mixes statuses and possibly death/injury/missing categories.
notes: Useful for explaining why low certified deaths and higher survivor estimates do not align mechanically.

citation_short: Official No Gun Ri Peace Park snippet on certified victims
title: 노근리평화공원 (search-result snippet on victim certification)
author_or_org: Yeongdong County / No Gun Ri Peace Park
pages_or_sections: Search-result snippet surfaced by web search
strengths: Official local memorial/government snippet stating the certified 150 dead, 13 missing, and 55 disabled/wounded among 218 recognized victims.
limitations: Underlying page timed out in fetch and the snippet is brief; not a full technical report.
notes: Used cautiously as the lowest documented death floor for No Gun Ri.

title: Battle of the Barricades: U.S. Marines in the Recapture of Seoul
pages_or_sections: Campaign and casualty discussion; text lines 93-94 and campaign loss lines from the PDF
strengths: Strong official source on military losses and urban fighting conditions in Seoul.
limitations: Not a civilian-harm database and does not provide a matched civilian denominator.
notes: Supports the proposed but not fully estimated Seoul recapture episode.
